summary: This cluster investigates the photoprotective role of mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) in marine macroalgae, particularly in response to increased solar UV-B radiation from stratospheric ozone depletion. Seven specific MAAs, including mycosporine-glycine, shinorine, porphyra-334, and palythine, were identified with absorption spanning both UV-B and UV-A wavelengths. Research demonstrated that Antarctic rhodophytes (red algae) with higher MAA concentrations exhibited greater tolerance to natural and artificial UV radiation, establishing a direct correlation between MAA levels and UV sensitivity. Experimental evidence further indicated that MAA formation is regulated by both ultraviolet radiation and photosynthetically active radiation (PAR), suggesting a complex physiological role beyond simple UV screening.
